deal-breaker: any role requiring frontend ownership over 20% of time

situation.real_reason_for_move: Waymo is narrowing focus to driver-only highway routes; his LLM-agent work for dispatch is being deprioritized. Wants to apply physical-world agent experience to digital agent ecosystems before skills atrophy.

deal_breakers: pure research role without shipping mandate

about_this_role.concern: digital agentic AI lacks the safety rigor he's used to; worried about sloppy eval culture
role may require too much web-stack work where he has gaps

about_the_company: valuation feels inflated relative to revenue traction
concerns.gut_feel: Respectful of the technical team but unsure if digital agents are as compelling as robotics.

life.constraints: two children in elementary school in Mountain View
life.external_pressures: spouse is considering returning to work part-time, increasing need for stability

volunteer: interested in sim-to-real transfer for agent evaluation
guarded: holding an offer from a quant hedge fund at $480k total
must exercise Waymo options within 90 days of leaving (cost ~$90k)
real concern is that the company's agent product is a chatbot wrapper and not agentic
will not relocate outside Bay Area due to family
requires immigration sponsorship (EB-2 I-140 pending) and cannot risk portability denial

situation.real_reason_for_move: Wants to move closer to product engineering but lacks clarity on domain; current platform team is shrinking and morale is flat

compensation_expectation.attitude: Would need a meaningful jump to leave a comfortable setup

deal_breakers: Early-stage startup without an existing SRE team

about_this_role.concern: Role seems focused on model-serving infrastructure he has never touched
Worried about AI being a hype label for standard DevOps

about_the_company: Unsure if they have a mature platform team
Heard they move fast and break things
concerns.gut_feel: Skeptical that they need an SRE or just want an AI engineer who knows Terraform

life.constraints: Young family, needs to be home by 6pm; cannot relocate outside the Midwest
life.external_pressures: Partner is expecting their second child in six months

guarded: Current base is $165k with a 15% bonus

Worried that AI model deployment is too different from stateless web services
Will not consider startups under 100 employees

situation.real_reason_for_move: Company went fully remote and dissolved the Austin office; she misses in-person collaboration and feels career visibility has dropped

compensation_expectation.attitude: Flexible if the team and product are strong

deal_breakers: Hybrid office requiring four or more days in office

about_this_role.concern: No mobile component mentioned; worried about pivoting to backend or AI

about_the_company: Unsure if they have a mobile app at all
concerns.gut_feel: This is probably a backend role that won't use anything I know

life.constraints: Bought a house in Austin in 2022; partner works locally
life.external_pressures: Planning to start a family within the next year

guarded: Current TC is $135k
Will not switch to Android or cross-platform

situation.real_reason_for_move: Curious about moving into data engineering or ML but lacks confidence in raw coding skills; feels plateaued as an analyst

deal_breakers: Roles requiring heavy statistics or model training

about_this_role.concern: Job description emphasizes LLM agent development, which is far beyond his skill set
Worried about coding interviews focused on algorithms

about_the_company: Seems very engineering-heavy; unsure if an analytics voice is valued
concerns.gut_feel: Might be interesting in two years after more engineering training, but not now
